description_en: Self-employed workers in Quebec must contribute both the employee and employer portions of QPP. QPP contributions are calculated on net self-employment income between the basic exemption and maximum pensionable earnings.

QPP2.description_en: QPP2 is an additional layer that applies to earnings between the Year's Maximum Pensionable Earnings (YMPE) and the Year's Additional Maximum Pensionable Earnings (YAMPE).

tax_deduction.description_en: Self-employed individuals can deduct the employer-equivalent portion (50%) of QPP contributions on their federal and Quebec income tax returns.

QPIP.description_en: Self-employed workers in Quebec are required to pay QPIP premiums, unlike in other provinces where EI parental benefits apply. QPIP premiums entitle self-employed workers to maternity, paternity, and adoption benefits.
